description: Guest Management MVP — CRUD for wedding guests with contextual fields for future AI-personalized invitations

userStories.title: Guest SQLAlchemy model and Alembic migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a guests table in the database with all fields required for the AI agent to generate personalized messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Guest model in app/backend/src/db/models/guest.py following the same patterns as User and Wedding models (UUID PK, Mapped types, mapped_column, server-side timestamps)

Fields: id (UUID PK), wedding_id (UUID FK to weddings, NOT NULL, indexed), name (String, NOT NULL), nickname (String, nullable), relationship_type (String, nullable), friendship_level (String, nullable), intimacy (String, nullable), contact_frequency (String, nullable), last_contact_medium (String, nullable), city (String, nullable), state (String, nullable), is_distant (Boolean, default False), memory (Text, nullable), shared_element (Text, nullable), ideal_tone (String, nullable), notes (Text, nullable), invite_status (String, default 'pending'), response_status (String, default 'pending'), created_at (datetime with tz, server default), updated_at (datetime with tz, auto-update)
All string choice fields (intimacy, contact_frequency, etc.) are free-text strings — no database enums
FK relationship to Wedding model with back_populates
Export Guest from app/backend/src/db/models/__init__.py
Generate Alembic migration via: cd app/backend && alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'add guests table'
Migration applies cleanly: cd app/backend && alembic upgrade head
mypy src passes from app/backend directory

userStories.title: Guest Pydantic schemas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need request/response schemas for the Guest CRUD API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create schemas in app/backend/src/schemas/guest.py following patterns from user.py and wedding.py
GuestCreate schema: name (str, required), all other guest fields optional with appropriate types
GuestRead schema: all fields including id (UUID), wedding_id (UUID), created_at, updated_at — use model_config with from_attributes=True
GuestUpdate schema: all fields optional (including name) for partial updates
GuestList schema: wraps list of GuestRead (e.g. guests: list[GuestRead])
Export schemas from app/backend/src/schemas/__init__.py
mypy src passes from app/backend directory

userStories.title: Guest service with CRUD operations and guest limit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer that handles guest CRUD logic, scopes queries to the user's wedding, and enforces a configurable maximum guest count.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MAX_GUESTS_PER_WEDDING setting to app/backend/src/core/config.py with env var MAX_GUESTS_PER_WEDDING, default 500
Create app/backend/src/services/guest_service.py following existing service patterns
create_guest(db, wedding_id, data): creates guest linked to wedding_id — raises 400 if wedding already has MAX_GUESTS_PER_WEDDING guests
list_guests(db, wedding_id): returns all guests for the given wedding_id
get_guest(db, guest_id, wedding_id): returns guest only if it belongs to wedding_id, else None
update_guest(db, guest_id, wedding_id, data): partial update, returns updated guest or None if not found/wrong wedding
delete_guest(db, guest_id, wedding_id): deletes guest, returns True/False based on ownership
All queries filter by wedding_id to enforce data isolation
mypy src passes from app/backend directory

userStories.title: Guest API router with CRUD endpoints
userStories.description: As an authenticated user, I can create, list, update, and delete guests belonging to my wedding via REST API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create app/backend/src/api/guests.py with APIRouter prefix '/guests' and tag 'guests'
POST /guests — creates guest using authenticated user's wedding_id, returns 201 with GuestRead
GET /guests — returns GuestList with all guests for authenticated user's wedding_id
PATCH /guests/{guest_id} — partial update, returns GuestRead, 404 if not found or wrong wedding
DELETE /guests/{guest_id} — deletes guest, returns 204, 404 if not found or wrong wedding
All endpoints use Depends(get_current_user) for authentication following auth.py patterns
Include guests router in app/backend/src/main.py
Rate limiting applied consistently with existing routers
mypy src passes from app/backend directory

userStories.title: Guest API automated tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests covering guest CRUD happy paths and the authorization boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file at app/backend/tests/ following existing test patterns and fixtures
Test: create guest returns 201 with correct data
Test: list guests returns only guests for the authenticated user's wedding
Test: update guest with partial body returns updated fields
Test: delete guest returns 204
Test: accessing a guest from another wedding returns 404
Test: creating guest beyond MAX_GUESTS_PER_WEDDING limit returns 400
All tests pass: cd app/backend && pytest
mypy src passes from app/backend directory

userStories.title: Guest list page with table, badges, and navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I can see all my wedding guests in a table at /dashboard/guests with status badges and a link in the dashboard navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Regenerate frontend types: run make generate-types (or scripts/generate-types.sh) so types/api.ts includes Guest schemas
New page at app/frontend/src/app/dashboard/guests/page.tsx (App Router)
Add 'Convidados' link to dashboard navigation
Table displays columns: Name, City/State, Intimacy, Invite Status, Response Status
Colored badges: Invite — 'Pendente' yellow, 'Enviado' green; Response — 'Pendente' yellow, 'Confirmado' green, 'Ausente' red, 'Incerto' orange
Each row has edit and delete action buttons
Delete button shows confirmation dialog displaying the guest's name
Empty state with call-to-action when no guests exist
Data fetched via api-client.ts (openapi-fetch) with types from types/api.ts
tsc --noEmit passes from app/frontend directory
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add/edit guest modal with sectioned form
userStories.description: As a user, I can add a new guest or edit an existing one through a modal form with fields organized into logical sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal dialog opens from 'Adicionar convidado' button and from row edit button

Form grouped into 3 sections with clear headings: 'Dados pessoais' (name, nickname, relationship_type, city, state, is_distant), 'Contexto para IA' (friendship_level, intimacy, contact_frequency, last_contact_medium, ideal_tone, memory, shared_element), 'Status e observações' (invite_status, response_status, notes)
Select/dropdown for: intimacy, contact_frequency, last_contact_medium, ideal_tone, invite_status, response_status
Text inputs for: name, nickname, relationship_type, city, state
Textarea inputs for: memory, shared_element, notes
Checkbox for: is_distant
Client-side validation: name is required, form cannot submit without it
Create mode: POST /guests on submit, modal closes, list refreshes
Edit mode: form pre-filled with guest data, PATCH /guests/{id} on submit, modal closes, list refreshes
API calls via api-client.ts with types from types/api.ts
tsc --noEmit passes from app/frontend directory
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
